meta.name: PQ-16 Prodromal Questionnaire
meta.description: The PQ-16 is a 16-item self-report screening tool for identifying psychotic-like experiences and ultra-high risk states for psychosis, abbreviated from the PQ-92 by Ising et al.
meta.author: Ising et al. (2012)
meta.reference: Ising HK, Veling W, Loewy RL, et al. The validity of the 16-item version of the Prodromal Questionnaire (PQ-16) to screen for ultra high risk of developing psychosis in the general help-seeking population. Schizophr Bull. 2012;38(6):1288-1296.
meta.instruction: Please read each statement below and indicate whether you have ever had such an experience. Select Yes or No.

questions.text: I sometimes feel that my thoughts don't belong to me
I sometimes have unusual sensations such as being touched, pulled, or pressed on my body
Sometimes I see things that other people can't see
I have had unusual or mystical experiences
I sometimes feel that my thoughts are known to others
I sometimes feel I can hear sounds that other people can't hear
I have felt that someone or some force is controlling my actions
I sometimes have difficulty concentrating because of too many unrelated thoughts
I have felt that there is a barrier between me and the world around me
I sometimes feel that TV or radio content is specifically directed at me
I sometimes feel that people are talking about me or watching me behind my back
I have had thoughts that made me think I might be going crazy
I sometimes feel there is something wrong with my thinking or speech
I sometimes feel that things or people are not what they appear to be
Sometimes my thoughts race so fast I can't keep up with them
I find it hard to understand what people are saying even when they speak clearly

ranges.label: Low Risk
ranges.description: You reported few psychotic-like experiences, indicating no elevated risk for psychosis at this time. Your perceptual and cognitive experiences appear to be within the normal range.
suggestions: No particular clinical concern is indicated at this time based on your screening results
If you experience any persistent unusual perceptions, intrusive thoughts, or feelings of unreality in the future, consider consulting a professional
Maintain healthy lifestyle habits including adequate sleep, regular exercise, and stress management as general protective factors

ranges.label: Attention Needed
ranges.description: You reported several psychotic-like experiences, placing you near the clinical screening threshold. While not necessarily pathological, these experiences should be monitored for changes in frequency or intensity.
suggestions: Pay close attention to the frequency, intensity, and duration of any unusual perceptual or cognitive experiences over the coming weeks
Maintain regular daily routines, consistent sleep schedules, and meaningful social connections as these support psychological stability
Minimize or eliminate use of psychoactive substances including cannabis and excessive alcohol, as these can exacerbate psychotic-like experiences
If these experiences increase in frequency, become more distressing, or begin to affect your daily functioning, consult a psychiatrist promptly

ranges.label: Screen Positive
ranges.description: Your score exceeds the clinical screening threshold, suggesting possible ultra-high risk for psychosis. This is a screening result, not a diagnosis, and requires professional clinical evaluation for proper interpretation.
suggestions: Consider scheduling an appointment with a psychiatrist or psychologist for a comprehensive clinical evaluation of your experiences
Early identification and intervention for psychosis risk have strong evidence for improving long-term outcomes and preventing progression
Maintain a healthy lifestyle including regular sleep patterns, balanced nutrition, physical activity, and supportive social relationships
Completely avoid all psychoactive substances including cannabis, stimulants, and hallucinogens as these can significantly increase psychosis risk
Share your experiences with a trusted family member or friend who can provide emotional support and help monitor any changes

ranges.label: Highly Positive
ranges.description: You reported many psychotic-like experiences, indicating a substantially elevated screening score. A comprehensive psychiatric evaluation is strongly recommended, as early intervention can significantly improve outcomes.
suggestions: Strongly recommend scheduling an appointment with a psychiatrist as soon as possible for a thorough clinical assessment
Early intervention services specializing in psychosis risk can provide structured professional support and monitoring tailored to your needs
Inform trusted family members or close friends about your experiences so they can offer support and assist with accessing appropriate care
Remember that endorsing these experiences on a screening questionnaire does not mean you have a psychotic disorder, and many people with elevated scores do not develop psychosis
